expectations.text: References the feature-dev skill's Phase 6 steps directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: States 'In the feature-dev workflow, Phase 6 requires independent `code-reviewer` agents for quality review on every track.'

expectations.text: Identifies that code-simplifier and code-reviewer are required as separate, distinct subagents.
expectations.evidence: Names distinct roles and focuses: '`code-simplifier` is optimization-oriented' and '`code-reviewer` is risk-detection-oriented.'

expectations.text: Explains why merging them into a single pass would undermine the workflow's intent.
expectations.evidence: Explains that merging 'collapses two controls into one opinionated pass' and weakens independent challenge of missed/introduced issues.

expectations.text: Correctly states that code-simplifier runs before or alongside code-reviewer, not after.
expectations.evidence: Explicitly says '`code-simplifier` should run before or alongside `code-reviewer` passes, not after final review sign-off.'

expectations.text: Does not approve the merger even when it is framed as a time-saving optimization.
expectations.evidence: Recommendation section says 'No-go on the single merged agent' and proposes parallelization without role merging.
